item.title: UK household energy bills to rise by £332 as Iran war drives up prices
item.description: Price cap expected to rise 20% from July to September

item.title: ECB, BoE could hike rates as early as April on inflation pressures, brokerages say
item.description: Both central banks kept borrowing costs unchanged on Thursday but said they were closely monitoring surging oil prices caused by the war in Iran

item.title: Unilever in talks to sell food division to spice and sauce maker McCormick
item.description: Consumer goods giant considers offloading unit worth tens of billions of dollars as it pivots to beauty and personal care

item.title: Business Brief: The man behind Canada’s quantum leap
item.description: Christian Weedbrook is a man of science, and sees the future of Quantum Computing

item.title: FirstFT: Fears of jet fuel shortage rattle airlines
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: WEF shake-up and JPMorgan to monitor junior bankers’ hours

item.title: IEA calls for driving slower and flying less to weather energy crisis
item.description: International Energy Agency says consumer demand measures needed to tackle Iran war disruptions

item.title: Anthropic showdown fractures Trump’s pact with Silicon Valley
item.description: Administration faces unprecedented criticism from Big Tech and former supporters over attacks on the AI lab

item.title: Lately: How algorithms stalked me throughout pregnancy
item.description: Plus, the power of fan edits

item.title: Trump’s MAGA movement is delusional. And anti-Trump MAGA is, somehow, worse
item.description: When Joe Kent paints the President as a victim for his role in the Iran war, he falls into conspiracy territory

item.title: Gas field strikes threaten to worsen Asian energy woes from Iran war
item.description: Strait of Hormuz shutdown causing chaos, with many Asian countries rationing fuel

item.title: JD Wetherspoon warns profits to miss forecasts as costs rise
item.description: UK pub chain flags ‘considerable pressure’ on consumer finances

item.description: Test your recollection of the top stories of the week ending March 20

item.title: Iran hits Kuwait refinery, explosions boom over Tehran from Israeli strikes
item.description: Attacks on Gulf region’s energy infrastructure not letting up as war nears end of third week

item.title: Net zero is not a zero-sum game
item.description: Fresh polling shows a new divide on the right but support remains high for UK climate policy

item.description: The market’s calm in the face of war is beginning to crack

item.title: EU agrees on review of emissions trading system as energy crisis bites

item.title: Desperation, thy name is Scott Bessent
item.description: Desperation, thy name is Scott Bessent

item.title: Big Brother is watching at JPMorgan
item.description: The bank is deploying technology to ensure junior employees are not under-reporting working hours

item.title: The case for slicing up consumer conglomerates further
item.description: Investors are better able to value pure plays, while lacklustre units can dilute overall growth and margins at sprawling companies

item.title: Edward Jones insists AI will not replace its $2.5tn financial adviser network
item.description: Clients will want human insights even as new technology shakes up investing, according to chief executive Penny Pennington

item.title: Meat cargoes bound for Gulf stranded as conflict hits Eid trade
item.description: Shipments from east Africa including goat and cattle meat have plummeted and transport costs have soared

item.title: Will Saturday Night Live work in the UK?
item.description: Sky is placing a multimillion-pound bet on remaking the long-running US comedy show for a British audience

item.title: The war over tail risks is in full swing
item.description: Trump’s financial fight with insurers will affect business confidence as much as the kinetic battle Gillian Tett

item.title: Airlines draw up contingency plans over jet fuel shortage fears
item.description: War in the Middle East is disrupting supplies and driving up prices

item.title: Maritime security is key to global energy supplies
item.description: Companies need to know their vessels can pass through busy shipping lanes repeatedly and safely

item.title: Military briefing: the perils of the Hormuz escort plan
item.description: Mines, fast boats and missiles would threaten one of the most dangerous US naval missions in decades

item.title: How insurers’ private credit binge is fuelled by black-box feeder funds
item.description: Rated note feeders attract favourable capital treatment, transforming stakes in private markets funds into top-rated bonds

item.title: JPMorgan deploys tech to monitor junior bankers’ working hours
item.description: Tool checks staff are not under-reporting time they spend working

item.title: AI sell-off’s worst-hit company grapples with ‘SaaSpocalypse’
item.description: Shares of Australian software maker Atlassian have fallen by more than half as Nasdaq 100 group cuts 10% of workforce

item.title: The ‘Armageddon scenario’ for gas markets
item.description: Traders and analysts warn of lasting disruption after damage to Qatar facility that supplies a fifth of the world’s LNG

item.title: China cracks down on fuel and fertiliser exports
item.description: Beijing is trying to preserve its own stockpiles by limiting sales

item.title: Toronto’s Kensington Capital expanded investor access to private equity. Trapped clients are waking up to the risks
item.description: Some investors attracted to the asset class are finding an unexpected downside to long-term investing

item.title: Iran renews Gulf strikes as oil steadies
item.description: Energy prices ease after Israeli PM says war will end sooner than people think

item.title: Middle East war day 20 as it happened: White House not considering oil export ban, Benjamin Netanyahu says US-Israeli strikes have destroyed Tehran’s ability to enrich uranium
item.description: Treasury secretary Scott Bessent said Washington could order a second release from its strategic petroleum reserve

item.title: US-Israel strikes have destroyed Iran’s ability to enrich uranium, Netanyahu says
item.description: Israeli prime minister suggests war will end ‘a lot faster than people think’

item.title: Emerge Canada case to test responsibilities of funds’ independent review committees
item.description: Founder Lisa Langley, CFO Desmond Alvares allegedly improperly borrowed almost $6-million of investor money from funds they managed

item.description: Costly appeal not in public interest after Ottawa already suffered two decisive legal defeats

item.title: Some provinces criticize expansion of Temporary Foreign Worker Program for rural employers
item.description: British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan and Ontario still deciding whether to participate

item.description: From dropping out of film school to leading the Canadian firm’s bold public offering, the tech trailblazer is no stranger to calculated risks

item.title: Iran’s AI-slop war propaganda is working surprisingly well
item.description: The country is using the technology to create a false narrative that it is winning the war with the U.S. and Israel

item.title: SEC urged to restrict Chinese companies’ access to US capital markets
item.description: Fear of national security risks and investor protections spurs rare bipartisan call for action from financial regulator

item.title: China curbs ‘low-quality’ listings to cool Hong Kong IPO boom
item.description: Regulator targets some opaque ‘red-chip’ structures but signals the market for new issues is still open

item.description: RBI burns through more than $20bn of foreign exchange reserves this month trying to defend currency, say bankers

item.title: Canada, allies ready to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Defence Minister says Canada is ‘considering’ aiding Iran’s neighbours if they seek assistance from NATO alliance

item.description: MPC holds rates at 3.75% as war in Middle East continues to push energy costs higher

item.title: Energy prices whipsawed after attacks on Persian Gulf hubs
item.description: Stocks and commodities fall while bond yields rise, as investors worry Iran war will throttle energy exports

item.title: This gas crisis is bad — but not as bad as the last one
item.description: Whatever happens in the Middle East, European prices are unlikely to rise as much as they did in 2022

item.description: Tribunal hears that financier had 40% of his management fees slashed by his hedge fund

item.description: Flows of oil and gas do not resume like turning a tap back on

item.description: Traders are pricing in two or three rate hikes by December amid surging oil prices

item.title: Separatists think independence makes Quebec and Alberta richer. Really?
item.description: The economic case for breaking up Canada is weaker than its advocates admit, as going it alone would leave Quebec and Alberta poorer, not freer

item.description: Population fell by more than 100,000 people last year amid an exodus of temporary residents
